findings.title: Three independent, undocumented 0.75/0.50 confidence-tier pairs exist alongside the canonical policy file
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (nutrition-engine). Verified all three hardcodes in current code: src/lib/planning/planImport/verifyImportRecipe.ts:41-46, src/lib/nutrition/aiLogging.ts:116-124, src/lib/recipe-ingredients/ingredientVerificationStatus.ts:70-78. verifyConfidencePolicy.ts is explicit that it is the single source of truth and its header (lines 33-36, 59-66) confirms ENG-746 piece 2 (raising the accept floor toward 0.70) is open — when that lands, none of these three sites move with it. Numeric agreement today is coincidence, not a shared constant. No live bug; pure drift hazard, and a well-evidenced one given ENG-1305 already fixed this exact class for the accept floor.
findings.recommendation: Extract DISPLAY_HIGH_CONFIDENCE=0.75 / DISPLAY_MEDIUM_CONFIDENCE=0.50 into verifyConfidencePolicy.ts and import from all three sites. No behaviour change. One Linear issue covering all three sites so a future accept-floor re-tune reviews them together. Route to executor.

findings.title: Untrusted-source ingredient row gets the green no-CTA "verified" tier at a bare 0.75 confidence score
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (nutrition-engine). Verified in ingredientVerificationStatus.ts: the fallback bucket (line 75) returns "verified" at confidence >= 0.75 when is_verified is false AND source is untrusted, and ingredientShouldShowVerifyCta (82-86) then suppresses the Verify CTA. Reachable in practice: an AI-parse row (source "AI"/"OpenAI import") with parse confidence >= 0.75 renders identically to a human-verified USDA row. The canonical policy file confirms 0.70-0.75 was never validated as a no-further-verification bar — the 2026-05-26 impact review only ratified it as display copy. Under the project trust posture ("Confidence is visible... never silently fill") granting the strongest trust treatment on a bare score from an unspecified source is an overclaim. This is a genuine do-not-guess case.
findings.recommendation: Escalate to Grace as a product decision (agree with the mechanical pass): either require an allow-listed source in addition to the 0.75 bucket before granting the no-CTA tier, or document with an impact-review citation why a bare 0.75 suppresses the CTA. My engine-side recommendation is the former — the fallback bucket should top out at "partial" (CTA visible) when the source is untrusted; the confidence buckets stay for the dot colour only.

findings.title: AI voice/photo logging flags (not rejects) low-confidence items — correct design, undocumented carve-out, plus a stale cross-reference comment
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (nutrition-engine). Verified: aiLogging.ts:106-110 claims the thresholds "mirror... the verifyIngredients tier classifier" — grep of verifyIngredients.ts confirms no 0.75/0.5 tier classifier exists there (only MIN_MATCH_CONFIDENCE*0.75 in a retry heuristic at line 711). The comment is stale. On the design question I concur as nutrition-engine: flag-not-reject is the CORRECT posture for free-text AI parses — there is no alternative candidate to fall back to, and dropping the item would silently under-count the meal, which is worse. The gap is documentation, not code.
findings.recommendation: Fix the stale comment in aiLogging.ts to stop citing a non-existent classifier (point it at ConfidenceDot / the display-tier constants once conf-1 lands). Add the explicit carve-out to CLAUDE.md's nutrition rules: AI free-text logging flags rather than rejects, with rationale. Route to docs-keeper.

findings.title: "Confidence" names five unrelated scoring systems with no glossary — plus an undocumented cross-system source-string collision
findings.detail: CONFIRMED and EXTENDED (nutrition-engine). The five-system enumeration is accurate. Adding what the mechanical pass missed: the same SOURCE STRING gets opposite trust treatment across two of these systems — "barcode" maps to the estimated tier in web NutritionSourceBadge (src/components/NutritionSourceBadge.tsx:10) but sits in TRUSTED_SOURCES → auto-"verified", no CTA in ingredientVerificationStatus.ts:46; "manual" renders grey Manual in log rows but auto-"verified" green in recipe-ingredient rows. Some of this is defensible (user-entered = ground truth for the user's own recipe) but none of it is documented, and it is the same-state-different-label class from the 2026-07-01 retro.
findings.recommendation: Write the module-level index of the five confidence systems in verifyConfidencePolicy.ts as proposed, and include a source-string trust matrix (which provenance strings map to which tier in which system) so collisions like barcode/manual are deliberate and documented. Route to docs-keeper + product-memory.

findings.title: FatSecret candidate matching evaluates only results[0] — no multi-candidate fallback
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (nutrition-engine). Verified in verifyIngredients.ts: line 962-963 `const best = results[0] ?? null` — only the top FatSecret hit is scored, versus USDA (ranked top-5, parallel top-2 + serial tail, lines 735-840), OFF (scored + sorted, 891-895), Edamam (iterates 5), Suppr user foods (3). FatSecret is last in the waterfall, so a failed results[0] means falling to the local estimator (confidence-capped guesses) even when results[1] would have matched. Direct violation of the generate-multiple-candidates invariant, on one source only.
findings.recommendation: Rank FatSecret's top-N with confidenceForMatch, filter to >= MIN_MATCH_CONFIDENCE, iterate in ranked order skipping zero-macro placeholder rows — same pattern as USDA/OFF, bounded inside the existing block. Route to executor; qa-lead to add the fixture from nutrition-test-1 covering fallback ordering.

findings.title: Edamam candidates iterate in raw provider order, not confidence-ranked
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (nutrition-engine). Verified: Edamam loop at verifyIngredients.ts:852 iterates edamamHits in provider order with continue-on-failure; USDA (735-747) and OFF (891-893) both sort by confidenceForMatch first. A weaker match that merely clears MIN_MATCH_CONFIDENCE at position 1 wins over a stronger match at position 3. Same fix shape as count-to-weight-1.
findings.recommendation: Score all Edamam hits, sort descending, then iterate — land in the same PR as count-to-weight-1 since both normalise the waterfall to the USDA/OFF pattern.

findings.title: Coarse per-piece weight buckets (5g nuts-to-shallots, 15g mushrooms-to-scallops) all earn "high" conversion confidence
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (nutrition-engine). Verified both halves: foodSpecificCountGramsEach (measureToGrams.ts:112-117) returns 5g uniformly for a bucket spanning almond (~1g) to whole shallot (~25-35g), and 15g for button mushroom through portobello; measureToGramsConfidence line 350 grants "high" for ANY foodSpecificCountGramsEach hit, identical to well-characterised eggs/chicken-breast rules. The doc comment (lines 80-85) confirms food-specific overrides BEAT size words, so "1 large portobello" still lands on 15g. Absolute kcal impact is modest for the low-cal produce cases, but the "high" label suppresses the low-confidence separate-rows fallback — the mislabelled confidence is the real defect, not the gram error.
findings.recommendation: Split whole-bulb shallot out of the nut bucket and split mushroom by named type when present; add a medium tier (or map many-food-bucket hits to "low") in measureToGramsConfidence so bucket coarseness is visible to callers. Route to executor; data-integrity to sanity-check the per-food weights added.

findings.title: Manual custom-food creation has zero Atwater/plausibility gate on either platform
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (nutrition-engine). Verified: customFoodsClient.ts contains zero plausibility references (grep); the dialog's save path gates only on canSave (create-custom-food-dialog.tsx:362/528/1055) and the AI scan's `implausible` flag only sets an advisory scanWarning (503-514, rendered 627) — never blocks save. The precedent hard-reject exists and works at app/api/user-foods/route.ts:142-158 (422 implausible_macros via scaledMacrosPlausible). A hand-typed 100 kcal / 50g-protein-50g-fat food saves silently and then feeds every downstream total at face value. Mobile shares the same ungated client write path.
findings.recommendation: Agree with the mechanical fix: server-side plausibility gate on custom-food create/edit matching the user-foods 422 precedent, surfaced inline in both dialogs. One nutrition-engine nuance: make it a hard-reject for Atwater violations beyond tolerance but keep an explicit user-acknowledged override path for genuinely unusual foods (matching the never-force-but-never-silent posture) — an acknowledged row should carry a flag, not pass as clean. Route to executor + data-integrity.

findings.title: AI meal-photo logging (/api/nutrition/photo-log) has no numeric plausibility check — the only ungated AI path
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (nutrition-engine). Verified by grep: zero plausibility/Atwater references in photoLogRanges.ts and none in photo-log/route.ts (the only "plausible" hit at line 88 is prompt copy). The sibling scan-label route wires checkMacroPlausibility with a documented 2026-06-11 rationale (lines 345-383) covering exactly this OCR/model-error class. A vision-model item whose kcal contradicts its own macro ranges by Atwater math passes through untouched at whatever confidence the model self-reports. Given Cal AI-class photo logging is a headline competitive surface, an unchecked hallucination here is a direct wrong-number-at-face-value risk.
findings.recommendation: Apply the scan-label pattern to parsed items: Atwater-check the midpoint (and flag when even the conservative bound fails), downgrade confidence to low + set implausible per item, surface before commit. Soft-flag, not hard-block — correct for photos of genuinely unusual foods. Route to executor; qa-lead to add fixtures with internally-inconsistent model outputs.

findings.title: USDA / Edamam / FatSecret search results are not plausibility-filtered — only OFF is
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (nutrition-engine). Verified by grep across app/api: plausibility gates exist only in user-foods and scan-label routes; the usda/edamam/fatsecret search routes have zero plausibility references, while OFF filters server-side in src/lib/openFoodFacts/searchProducts.ts:149-152 (isPlausibleMacrosPer100g, F-77). A physically-impossible vendor row is visible and selectable in search on both platforms for three of four vendors; the only catch is the non-blocking confirm-time warning. Vendor-parity note: OFF rows are the noisiest source (hence F-77), so the residual risk on USDA/FatSecret is lower-frequency — severity 3 as filed is right, not higher.
findings.recommendation: Agree: gate at the server routes (single choke point both platforms share), mirroring searchOffProducts. Route to executor; fold into the mp-F5 meta-test inventory when it lands.

findings.title: Four dead plausibility/search-trust imports in apps/mobile/lib/verifyRecipe.ts (post-ENG-1059 drift)
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (nutrition-engine). Verified: isPlausibleMacrosPer100g (line 34) and isBareGenericNounRow / isLowRelevanceNonVerifiedRow / isLowConfidenceDemotedRow (36-39) are imported and have zero call sites in the file (grep returns only the import lines). Not a functional gap — the /api/off/search route applies the identical check server-side — but the imports actively mislead a reader into believing mobile independently re-validates. Correctly rated low severity.
findings.recommendation: Delete the four imports in the same change as mp-F3 (same surface). Also verify the ESLint no-unused-vars posture on apps/mobile — this survived 3 weeks, so the rule is evidently not blocking there.

findings.title: No meta-test inventories plausibility-gate coverage — the structural reason mp-F1/F2/F3 shipped unnoticed
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (nutrition-engine). Verified tests/unit/nutritionEntriesGuardInventory.test.ts exists (the sibling coercion-guard inventory pattern) and no plausibility equivalent exists. The three confirmed gaps (custom foods, photo-log, three search vendors) are precisely the class such an inventory would have failed CI on. This is the highest-leverage single fix in the plausibility cluster because it converts a recurring audit finding into a write-time gate.
findings.recommendation: After mp-F1..F3 land, add the inventory meta-test: enumerate every path that persists or displays externally-sourced or user-typed macro rows, assert each calls a canonical plausibility function or is allow-listed with rationale. Also consolidate toward one canonical is-this-plausible entry point (four overlapping implementations exist). Route to qa-lead.

findings.title: RecipeUpload persists raw unfiltered estimator output into recipes.calories when verify hasn't completed — no accept floor, no trust signal, racing a silent background verify
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (nutrition-engine) — the most serious finding in the set. Verified: verifiedOk at RecipeUpload.tsx:1540; the fallback (1541-1548) sums raw estimateLineMacros with no MIN_ACCEPT_CONFIDENCE filtering, while the server pipeline it bypasses explicitly excludes sub-floor rows from totals (verifyIngredients.ts:1123-1145, ENG-691/ENG-1305). The background verify is debounced 700ms and silent (1462-1473), so save-before-verify-completes is a routine race, not an edge case. The persisted aggregate carries no confidence/quality column, so every downstream surface (rt-F3) renders it indistinguishably from verified numbers, and the RecipeDetail banner (rt-F2) can then caption it "Verified". This three-link chain is exactly Grace's named critical class: a number that looks authoritative but is silently estimated.
findings.recommendation: Apply the same accept-floor exclusion to the client fallback before summing; persist a recipe-level quality signal beyond boolean is_verified; gate Save/Publish on verify completion or an explicit "save with estimates" confirmation. Fix rt-F1/F2/F3 as one cluster — fixing the label without fixing the persisted aggregate (or vice versa) leaves the chain intact. Route to executor, with qa-lead fixtures for the save-before-verify race.

findings.title: RecipeDetail creator-discrepancy banner claims "Verified value calculated from ingredient data" without checking isVerified — and divides by zero when calories is 0
findings.detail: CONFIRMED, RE-RANKED 5→4 (nutrition-engine). Verified at RecipeDetail.tsx:2896-2908: the guard checks only isCatalogRecipe + creatorCalories + >10% divergence, never recipe.isVerified, then prints "Verified value...". Textual overclaim confirmed. Re-ranked to 4 because the trigger is narrower than rt-F1 (catalog recipe + creator calories present + >10% divergence), though it fires at the exact moment the user is deciding which of two numbers to trust. Still blocksRelease — one-line copy fix in a never-defer cluster. ADDITION the mechanical pass missed: the same guard divides by recipe.calories with no zero check — a 0-calorie computed aggregate with creator calories present renders "(Infinity% difference)".
findings.recommendation: Condition the copy on recipe.isVerified ("Estimated value calculated from ingredient data — not yet verified" when false) and guard the division (suppress the banner or the percentage when recipe.calories <= 0). Trust-posture note: even the verified branch should stay within the estimated vocabulary — "Calculated from ingredient data" is honest in both states. Route wording to legal-reviewer/ui-product-designer only if contested; otherwise ship.

findings.title: Recipe cards, discovery, planner and cook-mode render kcal with zero trust qualifier despite isVerified being on the data
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (nutrition-engine). Spot-verified the load-bearing anchor: DiscoverFeed.tsx:327 already reads recipe.isVerified for the (dead — see tl-F6) filter, proving the field is present on the exact objects the default render path shows unqualified; the RecipeCardWide/FeaturedHero/MealPlanner/CookMode greps from the mechanical pass are consistent with everything else verified in this audit. Combined with rt-F1 (unverified estimator numbers persisted into the same columns), every one of these surfaces presents an estimated number with verified-grade visual authority. Library's calories sort ranking unverified estimates against verified values on equal footing is a lower-order effect of the same missing signal.
findings.recommendation: Add the minimum honest qualifier — an "Estimated" micro-label or the existing dot/tier pattern — wherever is_verified is false on card/list/planner/cook surfaces, web and mobile in the same change. Route to ui-product-designer for the affordance, executor for wiring. Depends on rt-F1 persisting a usable recipe-level signal; sequence in the same cluster.

findings.title: Mobile create-recipe defaults missing source strings to "Verified" — inverted conservative-fallback, currently a landmine not a live mislabel
findings.detail: CONFIRMED, RE-RANKED 4→3 (nutrition-engine). Verified both sites: apps/mobile/app/create-recipe.tsx:135 (`r.source || "Verified"`) and 192 (`row?.source ?? (m ? "Verified" : "Pending")`), rendering verbatim to the user. The mechanical pass's own reachability trace (both branches effectively dead today because upstream always populates source) is credible and matches the response-shape code, so this is a future-shape landmine rather than a live mislabel — re-ranked to 3. The inversion itself is real and contradicts the documented conservative-label posture in the same codebase.
findings.recommendation: Flip both defaults to "Estimated" (or "Pending" where no macros). One-line ×2; land immediately with the tl-F1 badge consolidation since both fix trust-label fallbacks.

findings.title: NutritionSourceBadge classifySource diverges web vs mobile — FatSecret verified/green on web, Manual/grey on mobile; branch ORDER also inverts "adjusted" handling
findings.detail: CONFIRMED and EXTENDED (nutrition-engine). Verified both implementations. The divergence is worse than filed: (1) mobile lacks fatsecret/edamam/adjusted/quick/barcode branches entirely — FatSecret → grey Manual on mobile, green Structured on web; (2) branch ORDER differs — web checks adjusted/quick/barcode → estimated BEFORE the verified branch, mobile checks verified first, so a source like "USDA adjusted" renders estimated on web but verified on mobile (an over-trust divergence, not just under-trust); (3) mobile's `s.includes("off")` substring vs web's exact `s === "off"` are non-equivalent classifiers; (4) label copy differs ("Est." vs "Estimated"). Mitigating note: the headline FatSecret case fails toward under-trust (grey Manual), the safer direction — hence not a blocker, but the "USDA adjusted" case fails toward over-trust on mobile.
findings.recommendation: Agree fully with the structural fix: move the classifier into src/lib/nutrition/, re-export via @suppr/nutrition-core, keep only styling platform-specific — the same pattern as recipeTrust.ts. Unify label copy in the shared module. Ship with tl-F3's parity test.

findings.title: Mobile Today meal rows render a hardcoded verified-green dot for every non-thumbnail meal — SourceDot imported but dead
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (nutrition-engine). Verified: TodayMealsSection.tsx:1273 renders a plain View with backgroundColor: Accent.success regardless of meal source; SourceDot is imported (line 46) with zero JSX usages; formatMealSourceLabelForRow is prop-typed (155) and destructured (617) but never invoked. Web's twin correctly maps source → dot colour. This fabricates a verified-green provenance signal for AI-estimated and manual meals on the single highest-traffic surface of the primary (iOS) platform. Squarely Grace's critical class; blocksRelease stands.
findings.recommendation: Wire `<SourceDot source={mapMealSourceToDot(m.source)} size={6} />` into the no-thumbnail branch matching web, and either use or delete the dead formatMealSourceLabelForRow plumbing in the same PR (no silent deferrals). Visual before/after on sim required per project rules. Route to executor.

findings.title: NutritionSourceBadge test suite exercises only the web classifier — false cross-platform coverage
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (nutrition-engine). Verified: tests/unit/nutritionSourceBadge.test.ts:7 imports classifySource solely from @/components/NutritionSourceBadge (web); its FatSecret/Edamam-verified assertions pass while asserting exactly the behaviour mobile lacks (tl-F1). No test imports the mobile classifier. This is the mechanism that let tl-F1 ship.
findings.recommendation: Fix via tl-F1's shared-implementation consolidation (parity then holds by construction), plus keep a thin table-driven parity test that imports both platform entry points and asserts equal tiers — it guards against a future re-fork, not just today's drift.

findings.title: Same shared tier enum labelled twice on web — inline grid says "Partial match", verify modal collapses it to "Estimated"
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (nutrition-engine). Verified both maps: ING_TIER_LABEL (RecipeDetail.tsx:314-317: Structured/Partial match/Estimated/Unverified) vs TIER_STATUS_LABEL (recipe-verify-modal.tsx:83-88: Matched/Estimated/Estimated/Needs input) over the identical shared tier derivation. Sharpening: the two surfaces share the SAME dot colour map (TIER_DOT_VAR matches ING_TIER_COLOR), so the identical orange dot carries "Partial match" one screen and "Estimated" the next — colour says same-state, text says different, within one session. Mobile matches the inline-grid wording, making the modal the outlier.
findings.recommendation: Export one canonical TIER_LABEL record from ingredientVerificationStatus.ts and import it in all three surfaces. If the modal's action-oriented "Needs input" is deliberately kept for the unverified tier, document it inline per the same-element-same-treatment rule.

findings.title: Mobile-only Verified/Estimated confidence chip on AI log review — web dialogs never received the parallel treatment
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (nutrition-engine). Verified web side: zero matches for SearchResultConfidenceChip/redesign_search_results in voice-log-dialog.tsx — the code path genuinely doesn't exist on web, consistent with the mechanical trace of mobile's AiLogReviewItem chip behind the same flag that IS in sync for food search on both platforms. A parity gap in trust vocabulary, though web still shows ConfidenceDot + "AI estimate" (an honest, if weaker, signal), so this is divergence rather than over-trust.
findings.recommendation: Port the chip to web's voice/photo dialogs behind the same flag, or record an explicit decision that web keeps the simpler treatment. Route the choice through ui-product-designer; don't leave one flag name meaning different scopes per platform.

findings.title: Web Discover "verified" recipe filter is dead plumbing — no UI ever sets it true
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (nutrition-engine). Verified: filters.verified initialised false (DiscoverFeed.tsx:131-132), gate at 327, and the file's only setFilters call (1058) resets it to false — no toggle exists. Permanent no-op implying a feature that doesn't exist. Correctly rated severity 1.
findings.recommendation: Decide fix-or-delete: given rt-F3 recommends surfacing verified state on discovery anyway, wire the "Verified only" chip as part of that work rather than deleting — but either outcome needs a decision, not continued inertia.

findings.title: Multi-candidate ranking fallback (USDA top-2 parallel + serial tail) has no integration test
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (nutrition-engine). Verified: the fallback machinery exists (verifyIngredients.ts:747-840) and every mock in verify-ingredients-usda-mock.test.ts returns exactly one candidate (grep confirms single-hit mockResolvedValue shapes) — the fall-through-to-candidate-2 contract and the serial tail are untested. This matters more once count-to-weight-1/2 land, since those add two more fallback paths that would also ship untested.
findings.recommendation: Add the two fixtures as specified (top hit implausible → second accepted; both top-2 fail → serial tail). Extend to FatSecret/Edamam iteration once count-to-weight-1/2 merge. Route to qa-lead.

findings.title: "Ask for clarification" invariant — PARTIALLY KILLED: a clarification surface does exist (verify flow + review nudges); only a blocking pre-log prompt is absent
findings.detail: RE-FRAMED, severity 3→2 (nutrition-engine). The grep result is accurate (no "clarif" token outside landing copy), but the finding's conclusion — "no code path surfaces an ambiguous match back to the user" — is wrong. The clarification affordance exists under different names: below-floor rows keep best-estimate macros flagged for verification and are excluded from totals (verifyIngredients.ts:1118-1127), ingredientVerifyNeedsReview drives a recipe-level review nudge including an unconditional nudge on any excluded row (verifyConfidencePolicy.ts:118-152), and the verify modal / per-row Verify CTAs are exactly a user-disambiguation flow. What does not exist is a blocking ask-before-log interaction — which the CLAUDE.md rule ("only ask when uncertainty materially affects nutrition") frames as a constraint against over-asking, not a mandate to build a prompt. This is a wording-precision gap in the rule, not a missing feature.
findings.recommendation: Option (a) from the mechanical pass, with sharper text: amend the CLAUDE.md nutrition rule to name the shipped contract — ambiguous/low-confidence matches are flagged, excluded from headline totals, and surfaced for user verification via the review flow; a blocking clarification prompt is intentionally not built. No Linear feature ticket needed unless Grace wants a pre-log disambiguation UX as product work.

findings.title: Scoped nutrition test baseline — 696/696 green across web and mobile
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (nutrition-engine). Clean baseline accepted as reported (76 files, 696 tests, zero failures, both platforms). Standing caveat from this audit: green tests here prove the covered paths work — ntr-1, tl-F3 and mp-F5 establish that the specific drift classes found in this audit sit exactly in the uncovered gaps, so the green baseline must not be read as trust-machinery health.
findings.recommendation: No action. Re-run the same scoped filter after the fix cluster lands; the new parity/inventory/fallback tests from tl-F3, mp-F5 and ntr-1 should join this filter set.

findings.title: NEW: Plan Import confidence tier is computed from accepted-rows-only average — excluding junk lines RAISES the displayed tier while the totals silently omit those lines
findings.detail: Addition the mechanical pass missed, at the intersection of two findings it filed separately. Post-ENG-1305, verifyIngredients computes avgIngredientConfidence over ONLY the rows that cleared the accept floor (verifyIngredients.ts:1171-1182 — deliberate, so stats describe the summed row set) and exposes belowAcceptFloorCount (line 1199) precisely so callers can surface the exclusions; ingredientVerifyNeedsReview treats any excluded row as an unconditional review nudge. verifyImportRecipe.ts:41-46 ignores both belowAcceptFloorCount and minIngredientConfidence and derives its high/medium/low display tier from the accepted-rows average alone. Consequence: a 10-line imported recipe where 4 lines fall below the accept floor (excluded from supprNutrition entirely — headline calories understated) shows a HIGHER tier than the same recipe with those lines included. The trust signal is anti-correlated with completeness: more junk → cleaner average → "high" confidence on an incomplete number. This is the exact "looks authoritative but silently estimated/incomplete" class Grace flagged as critical, inside the Plan Import launch initiative.
findings.recommendation: In verifyImportRecipe, cap the tier when result.belowAcceptFloorCount > 0 (at most "medium", or reuse ingredientVerifyNeedsReview to force "low"/review), and consider surfacing excluded-line count in the import UI so the user sees which lines didn't make the totals. Small, isolated change; land with the conf-1 constant extraction since it touches the same file. Route to executor.

findings.title: NEW: FatSecret branch silently assumes a 100g serving when no metric weight resolves — per-gram macros can be off multiples at unchanged high confidence, and the plausibility cross-check is self-consistent so it cannot catch it
findings.detail: Addition the mechanical pass missed inside the block it audited. verifyIngredients.ts:995-996: `const servingG = servingMass ?? 100` — when servingMassGrams (fatsecretNormalize.ts:301-323) returns null (no metric_serving_amount and an unparseable serving_description like "1 serving" / "1 portion" / "1 package", which the measureToGrams 80g-catch-all guard deliberately rejects), the code treats the serving as exactly 100g and builds per-gram macros from it. A 30g snack-bar serving read as 100g understates per-gram macros ~3.3x; the downstream checkScaledLogPlausibility cross-check uses fsPer100g reconstructed from the SAME perGram (self-consistent by construction), so no guard fires, and confidence stays whatever the name match earned (can be 0.9+). Secondary: line 306 treats ml as grams (density 1) — acceptable for water-like liquids, ~8% off for oils; minor by comparison. Frequency is bounded (FatSecret usually ships metric amounts; this bites branded rows with description-only servings) hence medium confidence and severity 3 rather than 4 — but when it bites, it is precisely a wrong number wearing a high-confidence label with no flag attached.
findings.recommendation: Never silently assume 100g: when servingMass is null, either skip the candidate (fall through to the estimator, which at least carries honest low confidence) or apply a hard confidence demotion below MIN_ACCEPT_CONFIDENCE so the row is flagged and excluded from totals per the ENG-691 contract. Fold into the count-to-weight-1 FatSecret rework since it rewrites the same block. Route to executor; data-integrity to sample real FatSecret rows lacking metric amounts to size the exposure.

findings.title: RecipeUpload.tsx persists raw unfiltered estimateLineMacros() aggregates when verify never ran or failed — no accept-floor, no confidence, no trust flag
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (customer-lens review, code re-verified at src/app/components/RecipeUpload.tsx:1524-1548): when verifiedOk is false, chosenPerServing is the raw sum of estimateLineMacros() with no MIN_ACCEPT_CONFIDENCE filtering, upserted into recipes.calories/protein/carbs/fat; Save/Publish gate only on `saving !== null`, so a user can save before the silent background verify (line 1467) completes or after it fails. This is the root of the cluster and the purest instance of Grace's named failure: a recipe card then shows e.g. '540 kcal' that is a stack of 0.15-confidence staple guesses, indistinguishable from a verified number everywhere downstream (see fill-F3). The DB is_verified flag is honest (server trigger forces false) — the lie is created at the aggregate write + read layer. Severity 5 and blocksRelease upheld.
findings.recommendation: Apply the same MIN_ACCEPT_CONFIDENCE exclusion used server-side (verifyIngredients.ts:1118-1182) before summing the client fallback; persist a recipe-level quality signal beyond boolean is_verified; gate Save/Publish on verify completion or require an explicit 'save with estimated nutrition?' confirmation. Owner: nutrition-engine + executor; this and fill-F2/fill-F3 should land as one trust bundle.

findings.title: RecipeDetail creator-discrepancy banner unconditionally claims 'Verified value calculated from ingredient data' without checking recipe.isVerified
findings.detail: CONFIRMED, RE-RANKED 5→4 (customer-lens review; code re-verified at src/app/components/RecipeDetail.tsx:2896-2908 — exact text present, guard checks only isCatalogRecipe + creatorCalories + >10% divergence, never recipe.isVerified). Re-rank rationale: reachability is narrower than fill-F1 (catalog recipes with creator calories diverging >10%), but the word 'Verified' falsely applied is the single worst word to get wrong under Grace's 2026-07-01 ruling, so blocksRelease stays true — it is a one-line copy fix. ADDITIONAL edge found in review: the guard divides by recipe.calories with no >0 check, so a zero-calorie aggregate would render 'Infinity% difference' — guard `recipe.calories > 0` in the same change.
findings.recommendation: Condition the copy on recipe.isVerified: verified → keep current text; unverified → 'Estimated value calculated from ingredient data — not yet verified'. Add the recipe.calories > 0 guard. Owner: executor (S change), same PR as fill-F1.

findings.title: Recipe discovery/list/planner/cook surfaces (web + mobile) render kcal with zero trust qualifier despite isVerified being on the data
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (customer-lens review): 7+ render sites verified in evidence (RecipeCardWide web+mobile, FeaturedHero, discover-featured-hero, DiscoverFeed x4, Library incl. the 'Calories' sort ranking unverified guesses against verified numbers, MealPlanner day-total ring math, CookMode headline). This is the amplifier for fill-F1: the fabricated-looking aggregate is consumed everywhere a user decides what to eat. Customer-lens shaping of the fix: do NOT badge every card — the trust posture already says all nutrition is estimated, so blanket 'Estimated' chips would be noise. Put the qualifier where numbers drive decisions: MealPlanner day totals vs goal, CookMode headline, the Calories sort, and the north-star suggestion card (see cx-1); keep card treatment subtle (e.g. tilde prefix '~540 kcal' for unverified). Severity 4 + blocksRelease upheld given the never-defer cluster ruling.
findings.recommendation: Reuse the existing tier-badge/qualifier pattern from RecipeDetail on decision-driving surfaces first (planner totals, cook mode, calorie sort, north-star card), subtle '~' prefix on cards, wherever is_verified is false. Owner: ui-product-designer for treatment + executor; land with fill-F1.

findings.title: Mobile Today meal row: hardcoded success-green dot for every non-thumbnail meal — SourceDot imported but dead
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (customer-lens review; code re-verified at apps/mobile/components/today/TodayMealsSection.tsx:1272-1274 — plain View with backgroundColor: Accent.success, no SourceDot, no meal.source read; SourceDot imported at line 46 with zero JSX usage). Customer lens: iOS is the PRIMARY surface and Today is the highest-traffic screen; green is this product's success/verified colour language (calorie ring under-target = green), so an AI-estimated or manual meal wearing the same green dot as a USDA row is a fabricated endorsement, not a missing label — and a web+mobile user (Grace, N=1, uses both) sees coloured provenance dots on web and uniform green on mobile in the same list position. Severity 4 + blocksRelease upheld; fix is S and already 90% plumbed (import exists, prop threaded).
findings.recommendation: Wire the already-imported SourceDot + mapMealSourceToDot into the no-thumbnail branch matching web exactly; then either use or delete the dead formatMealSourceLabelForRow prop-threading per no-silent-deferrals. Owner: executor; verify with a fresh sim capture per visual-validation rules.

findings.title: NutritionSourceBadge.classifySource diverges web vs mobile — FatSecret renders 'Structured ✓' green on web, 'Manual ✎' grey on mobile
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (customer-lens review; mobile classifier re-verified at apps/mobile/components/NutritionSourceBadge.tsx:8-15 — no fatsecret/edamam/adjusted/quick/barcode branches, and substring `.includes("off")` vs web's exact `s === "off"`). Both error directions exist: FatSecret/Edamam/barcode entries UNDER-label on mobile ('Manual' — user thinks the app lost their source), while any 'usda…adjusted'-shaped string OVER-labels on mobile ('Structured ✓' via the usda substring, where web correctly demotes 'adjusted' to estimated). A dual-platform user sees the same logged food wearing different trust labels — the exact 2026-07-01 retro bug class. Severity 4 upheld; the re-export-shared-classifier fix is the correct root fix, not branch-patching.
findings.recommendation: Move the classifier into src/lib/nutrition/ and re-export via @suppr/nutrition-core (matching recipeTrust.ts/barcodeConfidence.ts pattern); keep only styling platform-specific. Land with label-F3's parity test. Owner: executor + sync-enforcer.

findings.title: AI meal-photo-scan (/api/nutrition/photo-log) has no numeric plausibility check — the only ungated AI path
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (customer-lens review): photoLogRanges.ts does only structural validation; no Atwater cross-check, no kcal/gram ceiling; per-item confidence is model self-report or range-spread tightness. Customer lens sharpens the harm: a hallucinated item can carry a 'High' confidence pill derived purely from the model reporting a TIGHT wrong range — confidently wrong is the worst presentation. Mitigation noted honestly: these items DO already wear 'AI estimate' / 'Estimated' labels (mobile chip, web badge), so the user is told it is an estimate — which is why this stays severity 4 rather than a blocker. The scan-label sibling proves the exact soft-flag pattern to reuse. Cal AI comparison applies: accuracy-questionable photo calories are the category's known trust sinkhole.
findings.recommendation: Run each parsed item's midpoint through checkMacroPlausibility-style Atwater + ceiling checks inside parsePhotoLogRangedResponse, downgrading confidence to 'low' and setting implausible per item exactly as scan-label does — soft-flag, never hard-block a photo of a genuinely unusual food. Owner: nutrition-engine.

findings.title: Manual custom-food creation has zero plausibility gate on either platform (direct Supabase write, no server layer)
findings.detail: CONFIRMED but RE-RANKED 4→3 with an amended recommendation (customer-lens review). The code facts stand: createCustomFood is a direct client write, canSave never checks plausibility, the scan-label 'implausible' flag is advisory only. But the customer-lens harm is different in kind from the rest of this cluster: these are the user's OWN typed numbers — nothing is 'silently estimated while looking authoritative'. The real harm is a typo (4500 vs 450 kcal) silently wrecking their day totals and making the app's math look broken. The mechanical rec of a hard server 422 would over-correct: real foods legitimately fail naive Atwater checks (high-fibre, sugar alcohols), and hard-blocking a user from saving the label they are literally holding reads as the app calling them a liar. The barcode flow's warn-once-then-confirm pattern is the proven right UX contract here.
findings.recommendation: Add the checkMacroPlausibility soft gate at save time in the shared write path (warn inline + require explicit re-confirm, exact parity with barcode.tsx / today-barcode-dialog.tsx pattern), applied identically web+mobile via customFoodsClient.ts. Keep the hard 422 only on the community-contribution route where other users consume the data. Owner: nutrition-engine.

findings.title: USDA / Edamam / FatSecret search routes return implausible rows to the results list — only OFF is filtered
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (customer-lens review): only the OFF route applies isPlausibleMacrosPer100g; web re-filters OFF-only client-side; mobile list has no filter. Severity 3 upheld — the confirm-time source-agnostic warning (foodSearchPreviewPlausibilityWarning) is a real backstop the user does hit before logging, so the list-level gap is a defence-in-depth + consistency failure rather than an unguarded write. Server-route fix (one choke point, both platforms) is correct over duplicating client filters.
findings.recommendation: Extend the isPlausibleMacrosPer100g filter to the USDA, Edamam, and FatSecret search routes server-side, mirroring searchOffProducts. Owner: nutrition-engine.

findings.title: apps/mobile/create-recipe.tsx defaults ingredient source label to 'Verified' on falsy upstream source — trust-inverted fallback
findings.detail: CONFIRMED but RE-RANKED 4→3 (customer-lens review; code re-verified at apps/mobile/app/create-recipe.tsx:135 `source: r.source || "Verified"` and :192 `source: row?.source ?? (m ? "Verified" : "Pending")`, rendered verbatim at :1171). Re-rank rationale: the evidence traces BOTH fallback branches as dead today (verify pipeline and image-import route always populate source), so no user currently sees a false 'Verified' — this is a landmine that inverts the documented conservative-fallback posture, not a live mislabel. It is a two-token S fix and must ride along with the other label fixes rather than ship as its own effort.
findings.recommendation: Flip both defaults to the conservative label (`|| "Estimated"` / `?? (m ? "Estimated" : "Pending")`) in the same PR as label-F1/label-F2. A fallback always degrades to the least-trusting label. Owner: executor.

findings.title: deriveIngredientVerificationTier grants 'verified' (green, no Verify CTA) to an untrusted-source row at bare 0.75 confidence
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (customer-lens review; code re-verified at src/lib/recipe-ingredients/ingredientVerificationStatus.ts:62-79 — fallback bucket returns "verified" at c >= 0.75 when is_verified is not true AND source is untrusted, and ingredientShouldShowVerifyCta returns false for that tier). Customer lens: green + no CTA is the UI saying 'nothing to check here' — the strongest do-nothing signal in the ingredient grid — granted on a bare score whose 0.70-0.75 band was never validated as a 'no further verification needed' bar (the canonical policy file documents 0.70 was rejected even as an accept floor). Severity 3 upheld. The mechanical rec is right that this needs Grace's call, not a guessed threshold.
findings.recommendation: Escalate to Grace as a product decision: should a bare confidence number from an unspecified source ever suppress the Verify CTA? Options per the mechanical pass: (a) require source allow-list for the no-CTA tier, or (b) document the 0.75 bar with an impact-review citation. Do not guess. Owner: nutrition-engine → product-lead.

findings.title: Three independent, undocumented 0.75/0.50 confidence-tier pairs alongside the canonical 0.55/0.50/0.20 policy
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (customer-lens review): three modules (verifyImportRecipe.ts, aiLogging.ts, ingredientVerificationStatus.ts) each hardcode 0.75/0.50 with no shared constant; they agree by coincidence. No live user-visible bug today, but the drift mechanism that produced the pre-ENG-1305 mobile/web accept-floor divergence is fully re-armed here — and when it fires it will fire as exactly the cross-surface label inconsistency Grace ruled critical. Severity 3 upheld.
findings.recommendation: Extract shared DISPLAY_HIGH_CONFIDENCE/DISPLAY_MEDIUM_CONFIDENCE constants into verifyConfidencePolicy.ts and import from all three sites; no behaviour change. One Linear issue covering all three sites. Owner: nutrition-engine.

findings.title: FatSecret candidate matching evaluates only results[0] — no multi-candidate fallback, unlike every other source
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (customer-lens review): evidence traces the full waterfall — USDA ranks top-5 with parallel top-2 + serial tail, OFF sorts top-5, user-foods checks 3, but FatSecret takes `results[0] ?? null` only. Customer impact is honest-but-worse quality: by the time FatSecret is reached every other source has failed, so a bad top hit sends the ingredient to the 0.15-0.35 local estimator (labelled Estimated, excluded from totals) when a decent results[1] might have resolved at 0.75. No silent over-trust — the user just gets weaker numbers and more 'Estimated' rows than the pipeline could deliver. Severity 3 upheld; bounded fix inside one branch.
findings.recommendation: Rank and iterate FatSecret's top-N with confidenceForMatch like USDA/OFF, skipping all-zero-macro placeholder rows, before falling through. Owner: nutrition-engine.

findings.title: Edamam candidate list not confidence-ranked before iteration, unlike USDA and OFF
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (customer-lens review): Edamam iterates provider order and accepts the first candidate clearing MIN_MATCH_CONFIDENCE, so a stronger candidate at position 3 can lose to a weaker one at position 1. Same honest-but-suboptimal class as count-to-weight-1, milder (a match above the floor is still accepted, just not the best available). Severity 2 upheld.
findings.recommendation: Score all Edamam hits with confidenceForMatch, sort descending, then iterate — same pattern as USDA/OFF. Owner: nutrition-engine, same PR as count-to-weight-1.

findings.title: foodSpecificCountGramsEach flattens 2.5-7x real-world weight ranges into single constants, all labelled 'high' confidence
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (customer-lens review): the 5g small-items bucket spans almond (~1g) to whole shallot (~25-35g); the 15g mushroom bucket spans button (~10g) to portobello (~80-100g); measureToGramsConfidence marks all of them 'high' identically to well-characterised cases (eggs, chicken breast). Customer impact is bounded — these are low-kcal-density items in most recipes and the number is at least deterministic — but a mushroom- or shallot-heavy recipe mis-weighs materially while wearing a high-confidence signal downstream. Severity 2 upheld.
findings.recommendation: Split the coarsest entries (whole shallot out of the nut bucket; mushroom by named type when a size word present) or add a third confidence tier for many-food-bucket matches so 'high' stops conflating coarse and precise rules. Owner: nutrition-engine.

findings.title: Same ingredient tier renders 'Partial match' in the RecipeDetail inline grid and 'Estimated' in the verify modal on the same screen, same session
findings.detail: CONFIRMED, RE-RANKED 2→3 (customer-lens review). Both consumers call the same shared deriveIngredientVerificationTier so the state never drifts — but the words do: inline grid 'Structured'/'Partial match'/'Estimated'/'Unverified' vs verify modal 'Matched'/'Estimated'/'Estimated'/'Needs input'. Re-rank rationale: 'the same thing called two different names on the same screen' is a per-se customer-lens fail — a user who taps into the verify modal watches 'Partial match' silently become 'Estimated' and reasonably concludes the state changed behind their back. Mobile's tap sheet matches the inline grid, making the web modal the unreviewed outlier.
findings.recommendation: Export one canonical TIER_LABEL record from ingredientVerificationStatus.ts consumed by all three surfaces; if the modal's action-oriented 'Needs input' is deliberate, keep it but document the divergence inline per the same-element-same-treatment rule. Owner: executor.

findings.title: Mobile-only Verified/Estimated confidence chip on AI voice/photo log review — web's parallel dialogs never received it
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (customer-lens review): mobile AiLogReviewItem renders the SearchResultConfidenceChip behind redesign_search_results; web's voice/photo dialogs have no equivalent code path (not a flag-off state — never written). The same flag IS correctly mirrored for search results on both platforms, which makes this an unfinished port, not an obvious deliberate divergence. A dual-platform user sees richer trust vocabulary on mobile than web for the identical AI-parsed item. Severity 3 upheld; fold the vocabulary decision into cx-2 before porting.
findings.recommendation: Port the chip to web's voice-log-dialog.tsx and photo-log-dialog.tsx behind the same flag — OR document a deliberate web-simpler decision. Decide alongside cx-2's canonical vocabulary call so the port doesn't propagate a word set about to change. Owner: sync-enforcer + ui-product-designer.

findings.title: Test suite exercises only web's NutritionSourceBadge.classifySource — false cross-platform coverage confidence
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (customer-lens review): tests/unit/nutritionSourceBadge.test.ts imports only the web classifier; its FatSecret/Edamam-verified assertions pass while asserting exactly the behaviour absent on mobile (label-F1). This is the mechanism that let the drift ship. Severity 3 upheld. If label-F1 is fixed by re-export, the parity test becomes trivially true by construction — still worth adding as the regression pin.
findings.recommendation: Add a table-driven parity test importing classifySource from both modules (pattern: recipeSourceCardParity.test.ts), landed in the same PR as label-F1. Owner: executor.

findings.title: No meta-test inventories macro-plausibility call sites — the structural gap that let plaus-F1/F2/F3 ship unnoticed
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (customer-lens review): nutritionEntriesGuardInventory.test.ts proves the pattern exists for the sibling coercion concern; no equivalent exists for plausibility coverage, and four overlapping Atwater implementations have no single source of truth. This is the fix that keeps the whole cluster from regrowing after the point fixes land — from the user's seat, it is the difference between fixing today's three holes and never meeting the fourth. Severity 3 upheld.
findings.recommendation: After plaus-F1/F2/F3 land, add an inventory meta-test asserting every macro-persisting/displaying path calls a canonical plausibility function or is explicitly allow-listed with provenance. Owner: nutrition-engine + executor.

findings.title: Multi-candidate ranking fallback path (USDA top-2 parallel + serial tail) has zero integration test coverage
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (customer-lens review): every fdcFoodsSearch mock returns exactly one candidate; the fall-through-to-candidate-2 path and the candidates-3-5 serial tail — the code implementing the 'generate multiple candidate matches' invariant — are untested, including the documented ordering contract at verifyIngredients.ts:760-762. Severity 3 upheld: a silent regression here degrades match quality invisibly, the same honest-but-worse class as count-to-weight-1.
findings.recommendation: Add the two integration cases the mechanical pass specifies (top hit implausible → resolves to candidate 2; serial-tail exercise). Owner: executor.

findings.title: CLAUDE.md's 'ask for clarification' nutrition rule has no implementation or product surface
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (customer-lens review), with a customer-lens steer on the resolution: the pipeline's three outcomes (accept / Estimated-excluded / Unverified) with visible review flags are arguably BETTER UX than a mid-log clarification interrogation — users logging a meal do not want a quiz, and the honest-estimate-plus-review-CTA pattern already covers the disclosure need. Recommend option (a) from the mechanical pass — scope the CLAUDE.md rule to describe the current no-guessing-without-disclosure contract — unless Grace wants a review-and-confirm step for belowAcceptFloor recipes specifically. Either way the rule cannot stay as written with no code and no ticket. Severity 3 upheld.
findings.recommendation: Put the (a)-vs-(b) choice to Grace as a product decision; default recommendation is (a) rewrite the rule. Owner: product-lead.

findings.title: AI voice/photo logging flags low-confidence items instead of rejecting — undocumented carve-out from CLAUDE.md's literal wording
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (customer-lens review), with emphasis that the behaviour is RIGHT and only the documentation is wrong: rejecting a low-confidence AI parse would silently drop food the user actually ate — flag-and-show is the correct trust posture for a pipeline with no fallback candidate, and it is implemented consistently cross-platform. Also confirms the stale aiLogging.ts:106-110 comment citing a verifyIngredients tier classifier that no longer exists. Severity 2 upheld — doc fix plus comment fix.
findings.recommendation: Add the explicit carve-out to CLAUDE.md's nutrition rules (AI free-text logging flags rather than rejects, rationale: no fallback candidate); fix the stale cross-reference comment. Owner: executor, bundle with nutrition-test-2's rule rewrite.

findings.title: Four dead plausibility/low-confidence imports in apps/mobile/lib/verifyRecipe.ts (drift from ENG-1059 refactor)
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (customer-lens review): dead since the 2026-06-11 proxy refactor; no live functional gap (server route covers OFF) but misleads maintainers into believing mobile independently re-validates. Zero user-visible impact; pure hygiene. Severity 2 upheld.
findings.recommendation: Remove the four dead imports alongside the plaus-F3 change; ensure no-unused-vars (or equivalent) blocks this class in CI. Owner: executor.

findings.title: Web Discover 'verified' recipe filter is dead/unreachable plumbing
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (customer-lens review): filters.verified is initialised false, gated at line 327, and no UI ever sets it true. Customer lens confirms severity 1 is right — the filter is invisible, so no user expectation is broken; it is a decide-and-delete (or finish) item under no-silent-deferrals. Note: if fill-F3's qualifier work lands, a working 'Verified only' filter becomes a natural companion — decide both together.
findings.recommendation: Either wire a 'Verified only' filter chip (sensible alongside fill-F3) or delete the dead state + gate. Owner: product-lead decision, executor implementation.

findings.title: 'Confidence' names five unrelated scoring systems with no glossary
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (customer-lens review): five distinct 0-1 signals share the name with only one cross-reference comment. This is the engineer-facing twin of the user-facing vocabulary sprawl filed as cx-2 — fix them together so the internal glossary and the user-facing word set map onto each other. Severity 1 upheld.
findings.recommendation: Add a module-level index enumerating the five systems, cross-linked from verifyConfidencePolicy.ts, written alongside cx-2's canonical user-facing vocabulary. Owner: nutrition-engine.

findings.title: Scoped nutrition/ingredient/confidence test run — all green, web and mobile (clean baseline)
findings.detail: CONFIRMED (customer-lens review): 76/76 files, 696/696 tests passing across both platforms; stderr noise verified as expected fixture output. Useful context for the whole stage: nothing in this cluster is broken math — every confirmed finding is a consistency, labelling, or gating gap sitting on top of a green engine, which is exactly the shape Grace's 2026-07-01 judgment predicted.
findings.recommendation: No action; re-run the same scoped filter after the fix bundle lands. Owner: executor.

findings.title: NEW: The north-star 'what to eat next' block scores and surfaces recipes with zero trust signal — an unverified estimator-fallback aggregate can be THE recommendation
findings.detail: NEW FINDING (customer-lens; missed by the mechanical pass, which stopped at planner/cook surfaces). Verified in code: NorthStarRecipe (src/lib/nutrition/northStarSuggestion.ts:63-80) carries calories/protein/carbs/fat with NO isVerified or confidence field, and grep for isVerified|is_verified|confidence returns zero hits in both northStarSuggestion.ts and recipeFitPercent.ts. The Today north-star block (D-2026-04-27-04, a permanent block on the retention surface) presents ONE authoritative recommendation — 'eat this, it fits your remaining macros' — whose kcal number can be an unfiltered estimator fallback (fill-F1) with no qualifier and no de-ranking of unverified recipes at equal fit. This is the product's single highest-trust moment consuming its least-trustworthy numbers invisibly. Note: I also checked the fit-percent pill for a related over-trust (synthesised fallback percent) and KILLED that candidate — F-45 (2026-04-22) removed the pill on both platforms; computeRecipeFitPercent is only void-referenced in DiscoverFeed.tsx:882 and discover.tsx:413, so no synthesised percent renders today.
findings.recommendation: Thread isVerified (and later a recipe-level confidence, per fill-F1's persisted-quality-signal work) into NorthStarRecipe: prefer verified recipes when fit is comparable, and show the same estimated qualifier fill-F3 introduces on the suggestion card. Ship as part of the fill-F1/fill-F3 bundle so the north-star moment is covered the day the qualifiers exist. Owner: nutrition-engine + ui-product-designer.

findings.title: NEW: Trust-vocabulary sprawl — a user meets 10+ distinct trust words with no glossary, and the flagship word 'Structured' is engineer-speak no zero-context user can decode
findings.detail: NEW FINDING (customer-lens meta-finding; the mechanical pass caught pairwise divergences — label-F4, label-F5, conf-4 — but not the aggregate user experience). Census across confirmed surfaces: 'Structured' (NutritionSourceBadge web+mobile verified tier, SearchResultConfidenceChip default verified label per apps/mobile/components/ui/SearchResultConfidenceChip.tsx:45-48, RecipeDetail inline grid), 'Verified' (create-recipe source line fallback + DiscoverFeed filter concept), 'Matched' (verify modal), 'Partial match', 'Estimated' and its abbreviation 'Est.', 'AI estimate', 'Manual', 'Unverified', 'Pending', 'Needs input', plus a separate High/Med/Low pill on AI review. A normal user cannot decode what 'Structured' says about their chicken salad — and the SAME internal tier is literally named 'verified' in code while rendering 'Structured', as other surfaces print raw 'Verified'. Possible nuance: 'Structured' may be a deliberate anti-overclaim choice (avoiding an absolute 'Verified' per the trust posture) — but if so it is undocumented and inconsistently applied, which fails the 'deliberately different and documented' rule either way. For a product whose differentiator is nutrition trust, the trust language itself is currently the least consistent vocabulary in the app.
findings.recommendation: Route to Grace + ui-product-designer + nutrition-engine as one product decision: pick a canonical 3-4 word user-facing trust vocabulary (e.g. Source-verified / Estimated / Needs review), decide the 'Structured' vs 'Verified' question explicitly, export ONE shared label map (alongside deriveIngredientVerificationTier) and migrate every surface onto it — sequenced BEFORE the label-F4/label-F5 copy consolidations so those fixes land the final words, not another interim set.
findings.route: all trust-label surfaces (search, barcode, AI review, recipe detail, verify modal, create-recipe, Today rows)
